title: podman fails to push manifest-lists to quay.io
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

manifest-list images created with podman fail to get pushed to quay.io

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Follow the steps in the [docs](http://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-manifest-create.1.html) with arbitrary images from quay.io, e.g.

```console
$ podman manifest create --all olm-list:v1.0 docker://quay.io/operator-framework/olm
```

2. attempt to push the manifest list to quay.io

```
$ podman manifest push olm-list:v1.0 docker://quay.io/dmesser/olm-list:v1.0
```


**Describe the results you received:**

```
podman manifest push olm-list:v1.0 docker://quay.io/dmesser/olm-list:v1.0
Getting image list signatures
Copying 0 of 1 images in list
Writing manifest list to image destination
Error: error pushing manifest olm-list:v1.0 to docker://quay.io/dmesser/olm-list:v1.0: Uploading manifest list failed, attempted the following formats: application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json(Error uploading manifest v1.0 to quay.io/dmesser/olm-list: manifest invalid: manifest invalid), application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json(Error uploading manifest v1.0 to quay.io/dmesser/olm-list: manifest invalid: manifest invalid)
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

Successful push. The same approach works with docker:

```console
$ docker manifest create quay.io/dmesser/olm-list:v1.0 quay.io/operator-framework/olm:v0.17.0 quay.io/operator-framework/olm:0.16.1 quay.io/operator-framework/olm:0.16.0
$ docker manifest push quay.io/dmesser/olm-list:v1.0

Pushed ref quay.io/dmesser/olm-list@sha256:bd73865b9840cf90ed558ddfcf51a9ba313b53598cc69c9b206669c883c972ec with digest: sha256:bd73865b9840cf90ed558ddfcf51a9ba313b53598cc69c9b206669c883c972ec
Pushed ref quay.io/dmesser/olm-list@sha256:b9d011c0fbfb65b387904f8fafc47ee1a9479d28d395473341288ee126ed993b with digest: sha256:b9d011c0fbfb65b387904f8fafc47ee1a9479d28d395473341288ee126ed993b
Pushed ref quay.io/dmesser/olm-list@sha256:de396b540b82219812061d0d753440d5655250c621c753ed1dc67d6154741607 with digest: sha256:de396b540b82219812061d0d753440d5655250c621c753ed1dc67d6154741607
sha256:87706e727538328a1fdb423a697547a041782e9b0ea93db12f54ef47a7ec5d38
```
**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.1.1
API Version:  2.0.0
Go Version:   go1.13.15
Built:        Fri Oct  2 14:30:39 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
No luck

```console
$ podman manifest push olm-list:v1.1 docker://quay.io/dmesser/olm-list:v1.1 --format v2s2
Getting image list signatures
Copying 0 of 1 images in list
Writing manifest list to image destination
Error: error pushing manifest olm-list:v1.1 to docker://quay.io/dmesser/olm-list:v1.1: Uploading manifest list failed, attempted the following formats: application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json(Error uploading manifest v1.1 to quay.io/dmesser/olm-list: manifest invalid: manifest invalid)

$ podman manifest push olm-list:v1.1 docker://quay.io/dmesser/olm-list:v1.1 --format oci
Getting image list signatures
Copying 0 of 1 images in list
Writing manifest list to image destination
Error: error pushing manifest olm-list:v1.1 to docker://quay.io/dmesser/olm-list:v1.1: Uploading manifest list failed, attempted the following formats: application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json(Error uploading manifest v1.1 to quay.io/dmesser/olm-list: manifest invalid: manifest invalid)
```
@dmesser what is the output of: `podman manifest inspect olm-list:v1.1` ?

Looks like the referencing manifest ( `sha256:9d8f18` ) have to be in the same org/repo.

```
$ podman version
Version:      2.0.5
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14.7
Built:        Wed Sep 23 18:18:02 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

$ podman manifest create olm-list:v1.0
55207bfee777e45e3c6b1c7f6f8d3b92e556f45bef80867495c966a8047c920e

$ podman manifest add olm-list:v1.0 docker://quay.io/operator-framework/olm@sha256:9d8f1880dc3bbb4174402316e29cd43790645f9788328f2df3afe57ce2bc8b57
55207bfee777e45e3c6b1c7f6f8d3b92e556f45bef80867495c966a8047c920e

$ podman manifest inspect  olm-list:v1.0
{
    "schemaVersion": 2,
    "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json",
    "manifests": [
        {
            "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json",
            "size": 1376,
            "digest": "sha256:9d8f1880dc3bbb4174402316e29cd43790645f9788328f2df3afe57ce2bc8b57",
            "platform": {
                "architecture": "amd64",
                "os": "linux"
            }
        }
    ]
}

$ podman manifest push olm-list:v1.0 docker://quay.io/rbo/olm-list:v1.0
Getting image list signatures
Copying 0 of 1 images in list
Writing manifest list to image destination
Error: error pushing manifest olm-list:v1.0 to docker://quay.io/rbo/olm-list:v1.0: Uploading manifest list failed, attempted the following formats: application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json(Error uploading manifest v1.0 to quay.io/rbo/olm-list: manifest invalid: manifest invalid), application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json(Error uploading manifest v1.0 to quay.io/rbo/olm-list: manifest invalid: manifest invalid)

$ skopeo copy docker://quay.io/operator-framework/olm@sha256:9d8f1880dc3bbb4174402316e29cd43790645f9788328f2df3afe57ce2bc8b57 docker://quay.io/rbo/olm-list:latest
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 9e87d4aff56d skipped: already exists
Copying blob 5e4c78272d5f skipped: already exists
Copying blob 2d11a1a9ab7e skipped: already exists
Copying blob eb402af95b59 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 188c0c94c7c5 [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 0.0b
Copying config 4ee32bdefb done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures

$ podman manifest push olm-list:v1.0 docker://quay.io/rbo/olm-list:v1.0
Getting image list signatures
Copying 0 of 1 images in list
Writing manifest list to image destination
Storing list signatures
```
Yeah I suspected something like this. It does however not explain why it works with `docker`.

Ah sorry got it, it looks like docker is pushing the ref upfront. Let's wait for more information from engineering :-) 

Last times @mtrmac  has very depths answers about it. 
 

@dmesser  Will add `--all` to the following push run success?
```
$ podman manifest push olm-list:v1.0 docker://quay.io/dmesser/olm-list:v1.0
```

@vrothberg I can confirm using `--add` works. I have to use it for both `podman manifest create` and `podman manifest push`. I think there is potential for simplification here by making `--all` implicit, at least for `push`.
